message:
feat(fnp): port 6 bitwise ufuncs to native Rust

Continue the NumPy-passthrough → native-Rust port pattern onto
the bitwise ufunc family. Six ufuncs land here in one commit:

Bitwise ufuncs (binary):
  - bitwise_and    (x1 & x2)
  - bitwise_or     (x1 | x2)
  - bitwise_xor    (x1 ^ x2)
  - left_shift     (x1 << x2)
  - right_shift    (x1 >> x2)

Bitwise ufuncs (unary):
  - invert         (~x, bit-flip)

fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs (~36 LOC)
------------------------------
Each new ufunc broadcasts via UFuncArray::broadcast_arrays and
emits a result of the broadcasted shape. Bitwise operations are
defined on integer types only, so the implementation extracts the
integer_sidecar path (or rejects with UFuncError::Unsupported when
called on a float input), runs the bitwise operation as i64
intermediates, and emits the result with integer_sidecar = Some(_)
preserved.

Behavior matches NumPy's semantics including:
  - NumPy treats Python booleans as 1-bit ints under bitwise ops
    (bitwise_and on bools is logical_and; bitwise_or on bools is
    logical_or). The native path mirrors that — bool inputs go
    through with no width promotion.
  - left_shift / right_shift mask the shift count modulo the
    bit width per NumPy convention; shifts beyond the width are
    well-defined (zero-fill for left, sign-fill for arithmetic
    right) rather than UB.
  - invert on signed types follows two's-complement (~x = -x - 1).

fnp-python/src/lib.rs (~110 LOC)
--------------------------------
Six new dispatcher wrappers + six #[pyfunction] entry-point
repoints, following the established `native_binary_*_or_passthrough`
/ `native_unary_*_or_passthrough` pattern. The fast path covers
the common 2-arg / 1-arg case; calls with kwargs (out=, where=,
casting=, ...) fall through to NumPy unchanged.

This brings the bitwise surface to native parity for the common
case. The combined (bitwise + comparison + logical + arithmetic)
fast-path coverage now covers the vast majority of the elementwise
ufunc traffic at the fnp_python boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
